wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Night Foodies project overview section. Explain the application's purpose as a late-night food delivery web platform operating between 11 PM to 6 AM, its core value proposition of providing essential items with instant delivery, and target audience. Document the key features including user authentication, real-time product browsing, cart management, and checkout process. Describe the technology stack combining Node.js backend with vanilla JavaScript frontend, the serverless-compatible architecture, and multi-backend data storage system. Include the business model, operational hours, and competitive advantages. Provide context about the application's design philosophy focusing on responsive glassmorphism UI and dark theme. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Night Foodies authentication endpoints. Document the four core authentication APIs: POST /api/auth/send-otp for generating and sending OTP codes to phone numbers, POST /api/auth/verify-otp for validating OTP codes and establishing user sessions, POST /api/auth/signup for creating new customer accounts with phone number verification, and POST /api/auth/login for authenticating existing users. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request body schemas, response formats, status codes, and error handling. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, success scenarios, and error conditions. Provide practical curl examples and JavaScript fetch API usage patterns. Document the complete authentication flow from initial OTP request through successful user session establishment. Address security considerations, rate limiting, and integration guidelines for frontend applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive storage architecture documentation for Night Foodies data storage system. Detail the multi-backend storage design supporting MySQL database, JSON file storage, and in-memory storage modes. Explain the automatic fallback mechanism that ensures data persistence across different deployment environments including Vercel serverless deployments. Document the storage mode selection logic based on environment variables (DB_DRIVER, CUSTOMERS_FILE, VERCEL) and configuration detection. Cover the factory pattern implementation for dynamic storage initialization, connection pooling for MySQL, and fallback strategies when primary storage fails. Include configuration options for each storage backend, performance characteristics, and deployment considerations. Provide examples of storage mode switching and integration with the application handler.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database configuration documentation for Night Foodies MySQL setup. Document all MySQL environment variables including DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, and DB_NAME with their default values and security considerations. Explain the database connection pooling configuration including connectionLimit and queueLimit settings. Detail the automatic database initialization process including table creation for customers and unique phone constraints. Document the MySQL driver configuration using mysql2/promise and connection string format. Include troubleshooting steps for common MySQL connection issues, authentication problems, and permission errors. Provide examples of proper MySQL setup for development and production environments, including Docker configuration and cloud database deployment options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Development Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive local development setup documentation for Night Foodies. Document Node.js version requirements, dependency installation using npm, and environment variable configuration. Explain the development server startup process, port configuration, and hot reload capabilities. Detail debugging techniques including console logging, error handling, and development tools integration. Cover development workflow including file watching, automatic restarts, and development-specific configurations. Document IDE setup recommendations, debugging configurations, and development best practices. Include troubleshooting common development issues, environment setup problems, and performance optimization for local development. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for different operating systems and development environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Input Validation and Sanitization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive input validation and sanitization documentation for Night Foodies application. Detail the validation strategies implemented for phone numbers, passwords, and personal information fields. Document the regex patterns used for phone number validation (/\d{10}/), password strength requirements (minimum 4 characters), and OTP validation (6-digit numeric). Explain the sanitization processes for trimming whitespace from user inputs and converting data types appropriately. Cover the JSON body parsing with error handling for malformed requests. Include examples of valid and invalid input scenarios, common validation failures, and their corresponding error responses. Document the security implications of each validation rule and how they prevent common injection attacks and data corruption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Send OTP Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the POST /api/auth/send-otp endpoint. Document the OTP generation and sending functionality including request body schema with phone number validation, response format with success/error indicators, and status codes. Explain the OTP generation algorithm, storage mechanism in the data store, and delivery methods. Include practical curl examples showing successful OTP requests and error scenarios like invalid phone numbers or rate limiting. Document the integration with the data store for OTP persistence and the serverless handler pattern used. Address security considerations for OTP generation and protection against abuse.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Night Foodies. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements (24.x), npm setup, and system dependencies. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation via npm install, and environment configuration. Document the environment setup process including .env file configuration, database setup for MySQL, and optional configuration for different storage backends. Explain how to run the application locally using npm start, access the web interface, and verify successful deployment. Include initial setup verification steps, common setup issues and their solutions, and basic troubleshooting for first-time users. Make the content beginner-friendly while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get the application running.

wiki_catalogs.name: Request/Response Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Night Foodies request/response handling system. Document the centralized request routing and processing mechanism implemented in the app-handler module. Explain the serverless-compatible handler creation pattern, request parsing and validation, response formatting standards, and error handling strategies. Detail the middleware pipeline, static file serving with security validation, and dynamic route resolution based on HTTP methods and URL patterns. Include examples of request preprocessing, response transformation, and error propagation. Document the handler factory pattern used for creating API endpoints and the integration with the data store layer. Provide guidance on extending the request handling system and adding custom middleware. Address performance considerations, logging strategies, and debugging techniques for request processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Customer Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed customer data management documentation for Night Foodies. Document the customer data model including schema definition with id, fullName, phone, email, address, password, and createdAt fields. Explain normalization functions, data validation rules, and type conversion mechanisms. Cover CRUD operations for customer data including findCustomerByPhone lookup, createCustomer insertion, and data consistency guarantees across storage backends. Detail the customer record normalization process, duplicate phone validation, and error handling strategies. Include examples of customer data access patterns, query optimization techniques, and integration with authentication flows. Document data migration procedures, backup and recovery processes, and performance considerations for different storage modes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Mode Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed storage mode configuration documentation for Night Foodies multi-backend storage system. Document the DB_DRIVER environment variable and available storage modes: mysql, file/json, memory, and sqlite (legacy). Explain the automatic fallback mechanism that prioritizes MySQL, falls back to file storage, then to memory storage. Detail file-based storage configuration including CUSTOMERS_FILE environment variable for custom file locations and JSON file format requirements. Document the in-memory storage mode for development and testing with data persistence limitations. Explain the Vercel deployment considerations and why file storage is not persistent on serverless platforms. Include configuration examples for different deployment scenarios and troubleshooting storage mode selection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed production deployment documentation for Night Foodies. Document server requirements including Node.js version compatibility, system dependencies, and hardware specifications. Explain production environment configuration including environment variables, database setup, and security hardening. Detail deployment procedures for different hosting platforms including traditional servers, cloud platforms, and serverless environments. Cover performance optimization including process management, clustering, memory optimization, and static asset serving. Document monitoring setup including health checks, logging configuration, and performance metrics collection. Include deployment automation using CI/CD pipelines, deployment scripts, and rollback procedures. Address scaling considerations, load balancing setup, and database optimization for production workloads. Provide deployment checklists, pre-deployment validation, and post-deployment verification steps.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed authentication security documentation for Night Foodies application. Document the OTP generation algorithm using Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000) and the 2-minute validity period (OTP_VALIDITY_MS). Explain the secure storage practices for OTPs including expiration handling and cleanup mechanisms. Detail the authentication flow security including phone number validation, password verification, and session establishment. Cover the rate limiting considerations for OTP requests and the protection against brute force attacks. Document the error handling strategies for failed authentication attempts and their security implications. Include security best practices for production deployment, including environment-specific configurations and monitoring recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Verify OTP Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the POST /api/auth/verify-otp endpoint. Document the OTP validation process including request body schema with phone number and OTP code validation, response format with authentication tokens or session data, and status codes for success/failure scenarios. Explain the OTP verification logic, expiration handling, and user session establishment. Include practical examples showing successful verification, expired OTP scenarios, and incorrect OTP attempts. Document the integration with the data store for OTP retrieval and validation, and the serverless handler implementation. Address security measures for OTP verification and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Night Foodies system architecture. Describe the high-level design showing the relationship between the Node.js HTTP server, custom application handler, and data store modules. Document the layered architecture with clear separation between presentation (HTML/CSS/JavaScript), business logic (application handler), and data access (data store). Explain the custom HTTP server implementation, request routing patterns, and middleware approach. Detail the multi-backend storage architecture supporting MySQL, JSON file, and in-memory storage with automatic fallback mechanisms. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Document the serverless-compatible design principles, factory pattern usage for data store initialization, and strategy pattern for route handling. Address scalability considerations, deployment topology, and integration patterns with external services.

wiki_catalogs.name: OTP Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive OTP management system documentation for Night Foodies authentication flow. Detail the OTP generation algorithms, storage mechanisms using Map-based in-memory storage, and expiration handling with expiresAt timestamps. Explain the OTP lifecycle from generation through verification to cleanup, including security considerations and validation processes. Document the saveOtp, getOtp, and clearOtp functions, their usage patterns in authentication workflows, and integration with the request handler. Cover OTP expiration policies, cleanup mechanisms, and error handling for invalid or expired codes. Include examples of OTP flow implementation, security best practices, and debugging techniques for authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security configuration documentation for Night Foodies production deployment. Document environment variable security practices including credential protection, SSL/TLS configuration for database connections, and secure communication protocols. Explain OTP security considerations including expiration handling, rate limiting, and secure storage practices. Detail authentication security measures including password hashing requirements, session management, and input validation. Document database security configurations including user permissions, network access controls, and encryption settings. Include security best practices for production environments such as environment isolation, secrets management, and audit logging. Address common security vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies specific to the Night Foodies application.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive monitoring and maintenance documentation for Night Foodies production systems. Document health check implementation, server monitoring setup, and performance metrics collection. Explain logging strategies including structured logging, log rotation, and error tracking. Detail maintenance procedures including database backup strategies, data migration processes, and system updates. Cover alerting configuration, incident response procedures, and troubleshooting workflows. Document capacity planning, performance tuning, and resource optimization techniques. Include maintenance schedules, update procedures, and disaster recovery planning. Address security monitoring, vulnerability scanning, and compliance requirements. Provide operational runbooks, escalation procedures, and team coordination guidelines for 24/7 operations.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data protection and storage security documentation for Night Foodies application. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Night Foodies - Late-Night Delivery Web Application

## Project Introduction

**Night Foodies** is a modern, responsive late-night food delivery web application designed specifically for the 11 PM to 6 AM time window. The platform provides essential everyday items including food, drinks, and cigarettes with instant delivery capabilities.

### Purpose Statement
The application addresses the critical need for late-night food delivery services, offering a seamless experience for customers who need quick access to essentials during off-hours when traditional delivery services may be unavailable.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Provide 24/7 delivery service for essential items
- Implement secure authentication with OTP verification
- Offer intuitive product browsing and cart management
- Support multiple payment methods (UPI/COD)
- Ensure cross-platform compatibility and responsive design

### Target Audience
- Late-night workers and shift employees
- Students and professionals working late hours
- Anyone needing essential items during restricted delivery hours

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern Node.js backend with a pure JavaScript frontend architecture, utilizing a flexible data storage abstraction layer.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[HTTP Server]
    B --> C[Request Handler]
    C --> D{Route Type}
    D -->|Static Files| E[Static File Server]
    D -->|API Routes| F[API Handler]
    F --> G{Storage Mode}
    G -->|MySQL| H[MySQL Database]
    G -->|File| I[JSON File Storage]
    G -->|Memory| J[In-Memory Store]
    E --> K[Frontend Assets]
    H --> L[Customer Data]
    I --> L
    J --> L
```

### Component Breakdown

**Backend Layer:**
- HTTP server built with Node.js `http` module
- Centralized request handler with route-based dispatching
- Modular API endpoints for authentication and business logic
- Flexible data store abstraction supporting multiple backends

**Frontend Layer:**
- Single-page application architecture
- Modern CSS with glassmorphism design
- Local storage-based session management
- Real-time cart updates and validation

**Data Layer:**
- Multi-backend storage system (MySQL, JSON file, in-memory)
- Automatic fallback mechanisms for production environments
- Environment-driven configuration

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

- **Factory Pattern:** Data store initialization with automatic fallback
- **Strategy Pattern:** Route handling based on HTTP method and path
- **Observer Pattern:** State management for cart and UI updates
- **Singleton Pattern:** Centralized request handler instance

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Server Entry Point:**
- `server.js` - Primary server startup with environment configuration
- Initializes HTTP server and handles graceful error management

**API Endpoints:**
- `api/auth/login.js` - User authentication endpoint
- `api/auth/signup.js` - Customer registration endpoint  
- `api/auth/send-otp.js` - OTP generation and sending
- `api/auth/verify-otp.js` - OTP verification and login

### Core Modules

**Application Handler (`lib/app-handler.js`):**
- Centralized request routing and processing
- Authentication flow management
- Static file serving with security validation
- Serverless-compatible handler creation

**Data Store Abstraction (`lib/data-store.js`):**
- Multi-backend storage system
- Automatic fallback configuration
- Customer data persistence and retrieval
- OTP management for authentication

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Variables:**
- `DB_DRIVER` - Storage backend selection (mysql, file, memory)
- `DB_HOST`, `DB_USER`, `DB_NAME` - MySQL connection parameters
- `CUSTOMERS_FILE` - JSON file location for customer data
- `PORT` - Server listening port (default: 3000)

**Package Configuration (`package.json`):**
- Node.js 24.x runtime requirement
- Production dependencies: dotenv, mysql2
- Development scripts for local testing

### External Dependencies

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Night Foodies] --> B[dotenv v16.4.5]
    A --> C[mysql2 v3.22.1]
    A --> D[Node.js Runtime]
    B --> E[Environment Configuration]
    C --> F[MySQL Database]
    D --> G[HTTP Server]
```

### Integration Points

**Database Integration:**
- MySQL database with connection pooling
- Automatic table creation and migration
- Structured customer data schema

**Authentication Flow:**
- OTP-based verification system
- Phone number validation
- Session management via localStorage

**Deployment Integration:**
- Serverless API route compatibility
- Vercel deployment support
- Environment-aware configuration

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Server.js] --> B[App Handler]
    B --> C[Data Store]
    B --> D[Static Files]
    C --> E[MySQL Pool]
    C --> F[File System]
    C --> G[Memory Store]
    H[Frontend JS] --> I[API Calls]
    I --> B
    I --> J[Local Storage]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Authentication System:**
- Phone number-based login with password protection
- OTP verification for enhanced security
- Account creation with validation
- Session persistence across browser sessions

**Product Management:**
- Category-based product filtering
- Real-time search functionality
- Dynamic pricing with delivery charges
- Cart management with quantity adjustments

**Order Processing:**
- Multi-payment method support
- Order summary with tax calculations
- Delivery fee based on time-of-day
- Secure checkout interface

### Implementation Highlights

**State Management (`script.js`):**
- Centralized application state with reactive updates
- Local storage synchronization for cart persistence
- Real-time UI updates based on user interactions
- Cross-page navigation with authentication guards

**Responsive Design (`styles.css`):**
- Glassmorphism UI with dark theme aesthetic
- Mobile-first responsive layout
- Smooth animations and transitions
- Custom scrollbar styling

**Security Features:**
- Input validation and sanitization
- CSRF protection through referer checks
- Secure password handling
- Rate limiting for authentication attempts

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Guest
    Guest --> Authenticated: Successful Login
    Guest --> SignedUp: New Account
    SignedUp --> Authenticated: Verify OTP
    Authenticated --> Shopping: Browse Products
    Shopping --> Cart: Add Items
    Cart --> Checkout: Proceed
    Checkout --> Payment: Select Method
    Payment --> OrderPlaced: Process Payment
    OrderPlaced --> Shopping: Continue Shopping
    Authenticated --> Guest: Logout
```

**Performance Considerations:**
- Lazy loading of non-critical resources
- Efficient DOM manipulation with event delegation
- Optimized image loading with CSS backgrounds
- Minimal third-party dependencies

**Scalability Features:**
- Environment-driven configuration
- Multi-backend storage support
- Stateless API design
- Serverless deployment compatibility

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [server.js](server.js)
- [lib/app-handler.js](lib/app-handler.js)
- [lib/data-store.js](lib/data-store.js)
- [api/auth/login.js](api/auth/login.js)
- [api/auth/signup.js](api/auth/signup.js)
- [api/auth/send-otp.js](api/auth/send-otp.js)
- [api/auth/verify-otp.js](api/auth/verify-otp.js)
- [script.js](script.js)
- [styles.css](styles.css)
- [index.html](index.html)
- [login.html](login.html)
- [signup.html](signup.html)
- [checkout.html](checkout.html)
- [checkout.css](checkout.css)

</blog>
